channel.title: Chart Breakers & Star Stories
channel.itunes:author: Family TVR
channel.itunes:summary: Did your favorite artist just break a record or a heart? The charts are screaming, and we have the inside story you won't hear anywhere else.
channel.description: The latest music trends, chart updates, and star stories from Billboard and beyond. Stay updated with the Pop Culture.

item.title: Ella Langley’s ‘Dandelion’ Spends Second Week Atop Billboard 200
item.description:
[Upbeat music fades out, and the host begins speaking]
Host: "Welcome to the latest Billboard update. We've got the scoop on the top albums of the week. Ella Langley's 'Dandelion' is still reigning supreme, spending its second week at the top of the Billboard 200 chart. The album earned 106,000 equivalent album units in the US, with a whopping 97,000 of those coming from streaming.
[Short music transition]
Host: "But 'Dandelion' isn't the only album making waves. TOMORROW X TOGETHER has debuted at number three with '7TH YEAR: A Moment of Stillness in the Thorns', earning 69,000 equivalent album units. This is their eighth top 10 album, and it's clear their fans are hungry for more. The album's sales were boosted by its availability in over 20 CD variants, featuring collectible items like photocards and stickers.
[Short music transition]
Host: "Other notable moves on the chart include Justin Bieber's 'SWAG' rising to number five, thanks in part to his Coachella performances. The KPop Demon Hunters soundtrack is also back in the top 10, after two new vinyl variants were released for Record Store Day.
[Short music transition]
Host: "Morgan Wallen's 'I'm the Problem' holds steady at number two, while BTS' 'ARIRANG' drops to number four. Olivia Dean's 'The Art of Loving' falls to number six, but still earns a respectable 46,000 equivalent album units.
[Closing music starts playing]
Host: "That's all for now. The full Billboard 200 chart will be posted on April 28, so be sure to check it out. Follow us on social media for more chart updates and music news. Thanks for tuning in, and we'll catch you on the next one!" [Music fades out]
